questions.question: A metal object exposed to a humid environment starts corroding. Which of the following methods would be the most effective and long-lasting in preventing corrosion?
questions.option_1: Applying a single coat of paint
questions.option_2: Coating with a more reactive metal like zinc
questions.option_3: Wrapping the metal with a dry cloth to prevent moisture exposure
questions.option_4: Periodically wiping the surface with oil

questions.question: Consider the following chemical reactions: I: BaCl₂ (aq) + Na₂SO₄ (aq) → BaSO₄ (s) + 2NaCl (aq) II: Pb(NO₃)₂ (aq) + KI (aq) → PbI₂ (s) + 2KNO₃(aq) Which of the following correctly classifies these reactions?
questions.option_1: I: Double displacement reaction; II: Neutralisation reaction
questions.option_2: I: Double displacement reaction; II: Displacement reaction
questions.option_3: I: Displacement reaction; II: Double displacement reaction

questions.question: During the chlor-alkali process, which of the following statements is correct regarding the products formed at different electrodes?
questions.option_1: Sodium hydroxide and hydrogen gas both form at the anode.
questions.option_2: Chlorine gas is released at the cathode and hydrogen gas at the anode.
questions.option_3: Sodium hydroxide is formed at the anode and chlorine gas at the cathode.
questions.option_4: Hydrogen gas is released at the cathode and chlorine gas at the anode.

questions.question: How is the valency of an element determined when the outermost shell is nearly full?
questions.option_1: By the total number of neutrons in the nucleus
questions.option_2: By the total mass of the atom
questions.option_3: By the total number of protons in the nucleus
questions.option_4: By the number of electrons needed to complete the octet

questions.question: LPG (Liquefied Petroleum Gas) is stored in cylinders in liquid form because:
questions.option_2: It reacts with air to form liquid
questions.option_3: It is cooled and compressed under high pressure
questions.option_4: It is heated and expanded under low pressure

questions.question: n carbon dioxide (CO₂), 12 g of carbon (C) combines with 32 g of oxygen (O). If 36 g of carbon is used, how much oxygen will combine according to the law of constant proportions?
The reaction of sodium hydrogen carbonate upon heating produces:
questions.option_1: Sodium carbonate, water, and carbon dioxide gas during cooking
questions.option_2: Sodium hydroxide, oxygen, and carbon monoxide at high temperature
questions.option_3: Sodium chloride, water, and hydrogen gas under normal heating
questions.option_4: Sodium sulfate, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide when strongly heated

questions.question: To neutralise excess acidity in the stomach, one can take:
What happens at the boiling point of a liquid?
questions.option_1: Particles expand slightly and remain in liquid form.
questions.option_2: Particles remain fixed and do not move at all.
questions.option_3: Particles gain enough energy to change into vapour.
questions.option_4: Particles lose energy and move closer together.

questions.question: What is the product when ethanol reacts with sodium?
questions.option_1: Sodium ethoxide and hydrogen gas evolve during the reaction with ethanol.
questions.option_2: Sodium carbonate and hydrogen gas evolve during the reaction with ethanol.
questions.option_3: Sodium oxide and water evolve during the reaction with ethanol only.
questions.option_4: Sodium hydroxide and oxygen gas evolve during the reaction with ethanol.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a heterogeneous mixture?
Which of the following correctly describes the effect of consuming ethanol?
questions.option_1: Long-term consumption of diluted ethanol has no health impact on humans
questions.option_2: Small amounts of dilute ethanol cause drunkenness, whereas absolute alcohol can be lethal
questions.option_3: Absolute alcohol in any amount causes minor intoxication without severe effects
questions.option_4: Small amounts of absolute alcohol are safe, but diluted ethanol is always fatal

questions.question: Which of the following substance groups represents the non-biodegradable substances?
questions.option_1: Spoilt food, vegetable peels, used tea leaves
questions.option_3: Plastic bubble pack, regular milk packets, medicine plastic bottles

questions.question: Which reaction is NOT a displacement reaction?
Why does the palm feel cool when acetone is poured on it?
questions.option_1: Particles remain still and reflect the surrounding heat.
questions.option_2: Particles expand and trap air between skin layers.
questions.option_3: Particles release energy to the palm and condense.
questions.option_4: Particles absorb energy from the palm and evaporate.

questions.question: Why is a colloid considered stable?
questions.option_1: Its particles react chemically with the dispersion medium.
questions.option_2: Its particles dissolve completely in the dispersion medium.
questions.option_3: Its particles do not settle down when left undisturbed.
questions.option_4: Its particles can be separated easily by filtration.
